methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Southern Launch is a growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by Lloyd Damp. The valuation landscape comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace, though the broader sector contains 23 same-sector entities.

Standard triangulation methods encounter material constraints. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage and valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector median unreliable. Public-comp anchoring, the alternative method, cannot be executed because Southern Launch's revenue is not on file—a common limitation with private companies. The public Space & Aerospace sector trades at a median P/S multiple of 59.8x across the 6 ticker universe, but this multiple cannot be applied without disclosed target revenue.

The analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range at this time. Material data gaps—specifically Southern Launch's revenue or comparable financial metrics—prevent application of both primary methods. Any valuation estimate would require either: (1) disclosure of target revenue to enable public-comp anchoring, or (2) identification of at least three private peers with sufficient disclosed financials at an equivalent stage and valuation tier. Until these inputs are available, quantitative analysis remains suspended.
